title: TGPetFile
description: AI Instruction: This schema defines the structure for a Tokengotchi Pet (.json). You must generate a strictly valid JSON file conforming to this structure.

name.description: AI: The identity or name of the pet. Keep it short and thematic.

palette.description: AI: Optional palette for colors used in SVGs via var(--key). E.g. 'base', 'accent', 'glow'. Include this so SVGs can share variables.

additionalProperties.description: AI: Must be a valid hex color code (e.g. #FF0000).

menuBar.description: AI: The context for the Mac menu bar. All SVGs should ideally use viewBox='0 0 100 100'. IMPORTANT: The menuBar is scaled down drastically to a 22x22 system icon. Keep all shapes and movements centered and animations extremely subtle so they remain legible at tiny sizes.

dock.description: AI: The context for the main desktop/dock renderer. All SVGs should ideally use viewBox='0 0 100 100'. This renders into a larger 128x128 canvas, meaning animations can be much more expressive and take up a larger area.

svgs.description: AI: Array of SVG objects used in this context. Usually contains a 'base_svg' with groups that are targeted by animations.

states.description: AI: Array of top-level states (PetMode). Must cover at least 'idle'. Valid IDs are: idle, busy, waiting, completed, error.

id.description: AI: Unique identifier for this SVG. E.g., 'base_svg' or 'surprise_face'.

svg.description: AI: The actual raw SVG string. Omit this if you are just referencing an existing SVG id. CRITICAL SVG RULES: 1) ALWAYS use inline presentation attributes (e.g. fill='var(--base)') instead of CSS classes. 2) BANNED ELEMENTS: <style>, <text>, <animate>, <use>, <image>. 3) Arcs ('A') are linearly approximated; prefer cubic beziers ('C') for smooth curves. 4) Use viewBox='0 0 100 100'. 5) Add id attributes to <g> tags so they can be animated via KeyframeTracks.

id.description: AI: For top-level states, must be one of: idle, busy, waiting, completed, error. For substates under 'busy', must be one of: reading, thinking, writing, searching, planning, building, running.

animations.description: AI: List of animation variants for this state.

subStates.description: AI: Optional substates. Usually used when the top-level state is 'busy' to define specific tool phase animations.

id.description: AI: Unique ID for this animation clip (e.g. 'idle_breathe').

name.description: AI: Human-readable name for the animation.

description.description: AI: Detailed description of what the animation looks like.

duration.description: AI: Duration of the animation loop in seconds.

svg.description: AI: Optional override SVG object to use instead of the context's default. Can ONLY be used if 'tracks' are used, NOT with 'frames'.

tracks.description: AI: Keyframe tracks for animating SVG parts via CSS transforms (rotate, translate, scale) and colors (fill, stroke). Mutually exclusive with 'frames'. Provide either tracks OR frames.

frames.description: AI: Array of full SVG strings for frame-by-frame animation. Mutually exclusive with 'tracks'.

targetId.description: AI: The DOM ID of the SVG element/group to animate (e.g. 'left_ear').

keyframes.description: AI: The keyframes for this track.

time.description: AI: The time in seconds when this keyframe occurs.

sx.description: AI: X-axis scale multiplier (1.0 is default).

sy.description: AI: Y-axis scale multiplier (1.0 is default).

fill.description: AI: Optional. Override the fill color (hex or var(--key)).

stroke.description: AI: Optional. Override the stroke color (hex or var(--key)).

opacity.description: AI: Optional. Override the opacity (0.0 to 1.0).
